commands.review_summary:
This PR adds opt-in Codex app-server profiler flag handling, wraps Codex startup/thread timing behind that flag, caches Codex workspace directory setup, adjusts native hook relay activation/priority, and updates docs and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +370, Tests +110, Docs +44. Total +524 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction: this is a PR adding opt-in diagnostics/performance behavior. Source inspection confirms the touched Codex startup, thread lifecycle, and native hook relay paths, but I did not run a live Codex startup repro.

target.title: fix(codex): gate profiler timing and startup setup
target.body:
## Summary

This extracts the Codex profiler/startup portion of #85941 into a focused PR.

- gate Codex app-server profiler timing behind an explicit flag helper
- preserve thread startup setup ordering while reducing always-on timing work
- update native hook relay coverage for the startup/profiler path
- document the diagnostic flag
- keep the original contributor commit author: Keshav's Bot <keshavbotagent@gmail.com>

Split from #85941 so the Codex extension changes can be reviewed independently from reply delivery and Telegram work. Thanks @keshavbotagent for the original work.

## Verification

- `git diff --check origin/main...origin/codex/split-85941-codex-profiler`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex app-server profiler timing is flag-gated and startup setup keeps the expected lifecycle ordering.
Real environment tested: local source checkout on fresh `origin/main` split branch.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check origin/main...origin/codex/split-85941-codex-profiler`.
Evidence after fix: split branch cherry-picked cleanly onto current `origin/main`; whitespace/conflict sanity passed.
Observed result after fix: no diff-check errors; commit author preserved as Keshav's Bot <keshavbotagent@gmail.com>.
What was not tested: focused Codex extension Vitest and live Codex harness startup were not rerun after the split; CI should run the normal PR gates.
